items.summary: Add a `TopVibingSong` type to the backend with fields: id (Nat), title (Text), artistName (Text), genre (Text), artworkUrl (Text), streamingLink (Text), tagline (opt Text), rank (Nat). Store a stable list of TopVibingSong entries in the main actor. Expose the following update/query functions: `addTopVibingSong`, `updateTopVibingSong`, `deleteTopVibingSong`, `reorderTopVibingSongs` (accepts full ordered list of IDs), and `getTopVibingSongs` (returns list sorted by rank ascending).

Add artwork upload support for Top Vibing Songs. When an admin submits the form, upload the artwork image as an ExternalBlob via the existing file upload utility (`fileToExternalBlob`), store the blob in the backend, and persist the resulting URL/reference in the `artworkUrl` field of the TopVibingSong entry.

Add a 'Top Vibing Songs' management section to the Admin Dashboard. The section must include: (1) A form to add a new entry with fields — Song Title (text, required), Artist Name (text, required), Genre (text, required), Artwork Upload (required), Streaming Link (URL, required), Tagline (text, optional); (2) A list of all current Top Vibing Songs displayed as draggable cards that can be reordered via drag-and-drop to set rank order; (3) Edit and Delete actions per entry. Use `dnd-kit` or equivalent supported drag-and-drop approach consistent with the codebase.

Display a 'Top Vibing Songs' section on the public Landing Page. Fetch entries via `getTopVibingSongs` and render them ordered by rank. Each card should show: artwork image, song title, artist name, genre, tagline (if present), and a 'Listen Now' button that opens the streaming link in a new tab. Style consistently with the existing MostVibingArtistsCarousel aesthetic.

Display the same 'Top Vibing Songs' section inside the User Dashboard. Reuse the same ranked card layout as the Landing Page. Place it as a distinct tab or section within the User Dashboard so authenticated users can browse the curated vibing songs.
